titleStmt.title: The United States Deputy Representative at the United Nations (Gross) to the Department of State

head.hi: The United States Deputy Representative at the United Nations (Gross) to the Department of State

div.p: 913. For Hickerson (UNA). Suggestion by Kennan in Moscow’s No. 1966, June 9,Not printed (795.00/6–952). that Dept consider “discreet, informal contacts” with Sov re Korean armistice tends to confirm my own feeling discussed with you June 5 that such contacts carefully studied and timed might be productive and in any event would serve to establish record of effort which would be potentially useful in future negots with our Allies. Such contacts, made here or in Moscow, should safeguard two tactical principles: (1) Avoid direct or indirect commitment on our part, and (2) leave Sov easy exit so that they would, as Kennan suggests, be in a position to assure China in case of leaks or queries that they have had no discussions with us.

Possible methods of approach for Dept’s consideration might be: (1) Leading Malik (possibly at his SC dinner this month) into very general conversation on Korea during course of which POW subject could be raised in most casual manner, further conversation on issue being subject to response, if any, from him; or (2) similar approach to Zinchenko at Malik dinner; or (3) discussion in Moscow.
As to (3), I am of course not in position to make suggestions as to how or when suitable opportunity might arise or be created.
Advantage of courses (1) or (2) would be: (a) Contact would be of routine and “natural” character, and (b) any interesting development could be promptly followed up in similar routine manner.
